behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is the validator-to-orchestrator handoff: the system must both aggregate missing fields and block downstream modules. A unit test of only field-check logic can miss accidental downstream invocation. Not System: no real OS/network/subprocess is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with inputs missing multiple required fields, with downstream insurance/triage/scheduling adapters stubbed. Assert error lists all missing fields and assert no downstream adapter was called.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires module interaction (gatekeeping), not just pure validation logic.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure input-validation logic at a single function/object boundary with no required I/O or cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with representative valid/invalid values (future date, >150 years old, malformed string) and assert specific validation failures.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower level than Unit is not defined; test is fully local.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is contract mapping between service logic and API client payload/response translation. In-process stub HTTP/client fakes can expose wrong field wiring and status mapping. Not System: real network behavior is not required for this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire insurance service with a stub payer endpoint/client that records request payload and returns each status variant. Assert outbound fields are correct and internal status mapping is correct for all three responses.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers cross-module request/response contract without needing real infrastructure.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is set to pending and intake continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at error-propagation boundary between API client and orchestration flow. A simulated timeout/connection exception in the payer client is sufficient to verify fallback and continuation. Not System: real network outage conditions are not strictly needed to catch this logic bug.
behaviors.test_description: Inject payer client failure (timeout/connection error) in an intake run; assert eligibility becomes pending and subsequent triage/scheduling steps still execute.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs orchestration interaction, but can be reproduced with local fakes.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This checks payload assembly across config + patient input + model-client adapter. A stub model client that captures request payload catches real wiring omissions. Not Agentic: behavior does not depend on model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage service with known symptoms and rubric config using a capturing stub model client; assert payload contains both symptom details and rubric fields exactly as required.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates inter-module data handoff into the model call wrapper.

behaviors.description: Live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model decision quality/classification behavior; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide this failure mode. Not Workflow: only one agentic decision is required here, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage against live model on emergency symptom cases and assert urgency is high at an acceptable pass-rate threshold across repeated trials.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real LLM output variability and quality evaluation.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: The risk is parser/storage handoff: explanation may be parsed but dropped before persistence. Unit parsing alone can miss repository mapping loss. Not Agentic: this behavior checks field persistence, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage response (score + explanation) through parsing-and-save path with test DB/repo; assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation field.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs interaction between parser and persistence layer.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality grounding from a real model. Stubbed responses cannot detect whether the model actually references patient-specific symptoms/rationale. Not Workflow: single model output quality check.
behaviors.test_description: Call live triage on symptom cases and evaluate explanation for mention of case symptoms and explicit urgency rationale using rubric-based scoring/human adjudication thresholds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Directly tests real model-generated content quality.

behaviors.description: Appointment scheduling chooses first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: This depends on scheduling logic + calendar data access interaction. A local DB/repo fixture can reproduce availability ordering/SLA mistakes. Not System: no real external infra is necessary if shared calendar storage is test-local.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar with occupied/free slots, run scheduler for high and medium urgency, and assert selected slot is earliest that satisfies each urgency window.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module scheduling plus storage behavior is exercised end-to-end locally.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a time slot under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: The failure emerges under concurrent access to shared calendar persistence and transaction logic. This is beyond single-function unit scope. Not System: can be caught with real local DB concurrency/locking tests without external OS/network dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against test calendar DB with real transaction semantics; assert at most one booking succeeds for that slot.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-appropriate prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily message composition/content selection logic from provided inputs and template rules, with no required infrastructure to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter/composer with representative appointment/patient/visit-type inputs and assert all required fields and correct prep instructions are present.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior can be validated fully at local function/object boundary.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to deterministic stubbed triage, so correctness is orchestration/state handoff across modules, not model quality. Not Agentic is disqualified by the phrase "deterministic stubbed triage output."
behaviors.test_description: Run full intake orchestrator with stubbed insurance/triage dependencies (deterministic triage output) and test DB; assert stage order, data propagation, and successful completion artifacts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires multi-module wiring; no real AI decision dependence per behavior text.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end with live triage model in the loop; success depends on real model output being usable by downstream steps. Mocking would hide model-dependent failures. Workflow not required because only one agentic decision stage is specified, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline in production-like environment using real payer API integration and live triage model for a controlled real submission; assert automatic completion from validation through confirmation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly requires live model + full orchestration; lower levels would miss model-dependent breakage.
